title: Handoff-gate evaluator with HBR_HANDOFF_GATE event emission (Rust runtime)
goal: Implement the runtime handoff-gate evaluator that fires on every governed inter-role transition (refinement->coder, coder->WP_VALIDATOR, WP_VALIDATOR->INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR, INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR->ORCHESTRATOR). The evaluator re-checks the packet's HBR matrix for the rules whose evidence_kind is satisfiable at handoff time, emits a typed `HBR_HANDOFF_GATE` EventLedger row, and blocks the transition on any failure.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/hbr/handoff_gate.rs; HandoffGate struct + evaluate() impl; new event types in EventLedger; tests proving gate fires on each role transition.
unblock_conditions: MT-001 loader available; MT-003 matrix-check semantics defined so handoff-gate reuses identical row-status interpretation.
implementation_notes: Per spec §5.6.2 (AC-HBR-HANDOFF-GATE) handoff-time enforcement complements build-time enforcement. The HandoffGate primitive: `HandoffGate::evaluate(packet: &Packet, transition: HandoffTransition) -> Result<HandoffEvidence, HandoffBlock>` where `HandoffTransition` is the enum `{ RefinementToCoder, CoderToWpValidator, WpValidatorToIntegrationValidator, IntegrationValidatorToOrchestrator }`. The evaluator loads the packet's `acceptance_matrix.hbr` (via MT-001/MT-002 contracts), filters to rules whose evidence_kind is checkable at this transition point (e.g., `test_run_with_ledger_replay` is checkable post-coder; `concurrent_session_test_run` is checkable post-WP_VALIDATOR), and verifies each filtered row's status. On success, emit an EventLedger row `HBR_HANDOFF_GATE { wp_id, transition, gate_uuid: Uuid::now_v7(), evaluated_rules: Vec<{hbr_id, status, evidence_pointer}>, verdict: Pass, timestamp_utc }`. On failure, emit `HBR_HANDOFF_GATE { ..., verdict: Block { failing_rules: Vec<{hbr_id, reason}> } }` and return `Err(HandoffBlock)` which the caller (SessionBroker, see KERNEL-001) must honor — the transition does not happen. Per HBR-INT-008 the gate_uuid uses Uuid::now_v7(). Tests: spawn an in-memory EventLedger (KERNEL-001 primitive), construct a packet with mixed PROVED/PENDING rows, call evaluate() for each transition, assert pass when all relevant rules PROVED, assert Block + correct failing_rules list when any relevant rule PENDING. Add a test asserting that two consecutive `Uuid::now_v7()` mints satisfy `a <= b` (per HBR-INT-008 wire-test obligation).
risk_if_missed: Without runtime gate, role-to-role handoffs proceed even when HBR evidence is missing — defeats §5.6.2 hard gate intent.
validator_focus: Every HandoffTransition variant has a dedicated test; the EventLedger row is emitted on BOTH pass and block paths (block must still be observable post-hoc); gate_uuid is v7 per HBR-INT-008.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Block path MUST also emit an HBR_HANDOFF_GATE event so post-hoc audit sees why the transition failed.
Per-transition mapping of which evidence_kinds are checkable at that point must live in a single table referenced from the test, preventing drift.
gate_uuid is Uuid::now_v7() and a time-order test asserts monotonicity per HBR-INT-008.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: HandoffGate cleanly evaluates the matrix, mints Uuid::now_v7() for gate_uuid, emits an HBR_HANDOFF_GATE event with idempotency key, and returns HandoffBlock with the event present (per red-team control). However the checkable_evidence_kinds() table omits ALL HBR-STOP-* evidence_kinds (the 5 STOP rules added 2026-05-20) — STOP rules can never be evaluated at any transition so they will silently pass the handoff gate even when a packet matrix has them PENDING/BLOCKED.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: HandoffGate cleanly evaluates the matrix, mints Uuid::now_v7() for gate_uuid, emits an HBR_HANDOFF_GATE event with idempotency key, and returns HandoffBlock with the event present (per red-team control). However the checkable_evidence_kinds() table omits ALL HBR-STOP-* evidence_kinds (the 5 STOP rules added 2026-05-20) — STOP rules can never be evaluated at any transition so they will silently pass the handoff gate even when a packet matrix has them PENDING/BLOCKED.
